articles.title: Odisha rolls out new SOP to tackle extreme heat amid rising temperatures - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
In response to the intensifying heatwave across the state, the Odisha Revenue Department on Saturday introduced a new Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) aimed at safeguarding citizens during extreme temperatures. The new guidelines focus on setting up heatwave shelters in areas with elevated temperatures.
The new SOP empowers District Collectors to make critical decisions on heatwave management in their respective areas, ensuring swift and effective action.
The SOP specifies that the most intense heat period from 11 AM to 3 PM will require heightened vigilance. During this time, district authorities will ensure rest stations equipped with ORS (Oral Rehydration Solution) and drinking water are available for drivers and travelers.
Additionally, if anyone faces heat-related issues, ambulances are instructed to reach the affected individual within 20 minutes.
School Holidays and Compensation for Heat-Related Deaths
In areas experiencing extreme heat, district collectors are authorized to close schools to protect students. Moreover, the SOP promises compensation for families affected by heatstroke deaths, with compensation payouts made within 24 hours of a report being filed.
According to the SOP, if an AC-less vehicle operates in areas with heatwave or if anyone faces difficulties, the owner will be held responsible.
Through a video conference with District Collectors, Odisha Revenue Minister Suresh Pujari emphasised the importance of adhering to the new guidelines, urging officials to act swiftly and responsibly to mitigate the adverse effects of the ongoing heatwave.
"For a long time, the Odisha government has been making all the decisions, with District Collectors often sending their requests to the Revenue Department for approval. However, we have now empowered the district officials. Outside of the SOPs outlined by the Odisha government, if they deem it necessary to make specific decisions based on the circumstances, they are authorised to handle those matters and take responsibility. They can then inform the Odisha government accordingly," said Odisha Revenue Minister Suresh Pujari.

articles.title: Odisha Issues SOP to Strengthen Heatwave Preparedness Across State - Hindusthan Samachar English
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar, 18 April (H.S.): The Odisha government has issued a comprehensive Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) to enhance preparedness and response to heatwave conditions across the state, Revenue and Disaster Management Minister Suresh Pujari said on Saturday.
Addressing the media, the Minister stated that the SOP aims to ensure coordinated action among various departments while empowering district administrations to take timely decisions based on local temperature conditions and ground realities.
Under the new guidelines, district collectors have been authorized to implement necessary measures, with flexibility to respond swiftly depending on the severity of heatwave conditions. Such decisions will be subject to subsequent ratification by the state government.
As part of the proposed measures, the government is considering restrictions on vehicular movement during peak heat hours between 11 am and 3 pm in severely affected areas. Plans are also in place to establish roadside shelters for drivers and ensure the availability of drinking water and ORS packets to prevent heat-related illnesses.
The Minister further informed that dedicated heatwave shelters will be set up in vulnerable locations, modeled on existing cyclone and flood shelters in the state.
Emergency services, including ambulances and fire services, have been directed to respond to distress calls within 20 minutes. Additionally, central and state public sector undertakings (PSUs), along with industries located along national highways, have agreed to provide drinking water and ORS to commuters during the heatwave period.
The government is working in coordination with multiple departments, including Panchayati Raj & Drinking Water, Housing & Urban Development, and district administrations, to ensure effective implementation of the measures.
“All districts have submitted their preparedness reports, and we are fully equipped to handle the situation. Compared to previous years, Odisha is in a stronger position to manage heatwave conditions without major impact,” Pujari said.
---------------
Hindusthan Samachar / Monalisa Panda

articles.title: Heatstroke Death Aid Update | Govt Announces Help Within 24 Hours | Minister’s Big Statement - Kanak News Odisha
articles.full_text:
The Disaster Management Minister has announced that financial assistance will be provided within 24 hours in case of death due to heatstroke.
This decision comes amid rising temperatures and increasing concerns over heatwave-related health risks. The government aims to ensure quick support for affected families during extreme summer conditions.
